history: The client has a pattern where relationships sometimes become romantic, suggesting a strong drive for closeness. There have been conflicts in relationships after expressing anger, pointing to difficulties with emotional regulation and communication during disagreements. The client describes a complex relationship with their parents, marked by a sense of obligation, which may contribute to ongoing feelings of pressure and stress. These relational and familial dynamics likely shape the client's beliefs about themselves and their coping patterns, especially around expectations and fear of negative evaluation.
intermediate_belief: If I make a mistake at work, others will think less of me and I will let people down. I must avoid errors at all costs to prove my value. If I can't be perfect, I risk losing acceptance and respect from others.
intermediate_belief_depression: If I keep making mistakes or can't get things done, it means I'm a failure and nothing I do will ever be good enough. There's no point in trying if I can't meet expectations.
coping_strategies: The client copes by procrastinating and avoiding work tasks that feel high-stakes or anxiety-provoking, which provides short-term relief from fear but increases long-term stress and guilt. They may also ruminate about potential mistakes and seek reassurance or withdraw from challenging situations, reinforcing the avoidance cycle.
situation: Difficulty completing work tasks due to fear of making mistakes. Procrastination and avoidance impacting productivity.
auto_thought: If I start this task and make a mistake, everyone will see that I'm not good enough. It's safer to put it off than risk failing and being judged.
behavior: I put off starting the work task and distract myself with other activities. I avoid opening emails or working on assignments, hoping the anxiety will go away.
